1→---
     2→name: roadmap-review-checks
     3→description: Roadmap-specific validation checks for architecture reviews. Load when reviewing roadmaps for implementation readiness.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Review Checks
     7→
     8→Six mandatory checks for every roadmap review. Each produces a finding block.
     9→
    10→## Check 1: External Validity
    11→
    12→Verify completing all steps produces an INVOCABLE feature, not just existing code.
    13→
    14→Criteria: at least one step targets entry point integration | acceptance tests invoke through driving ports | clear user invocation path after completion
    15→
    16→Severity: BLOCKER if no integration step or invocation path. HIGH if tests at wrong boundary.
    17→
    18→```
    19→EXTERNAL VALIDITY CHECK: PASSED|FAILED
    20→
    21→Issue: {missing wiring}
    22→Consequence: {what happens without fix}
    23→Required Action: {integration step to add}
    24→```
    25→
    26→## Check 2: AC Implementation Coupling
    27→
    28→Scan AC for implementation details locking crafter into predetermined structure.
    29→
    30→Detection: underscore-prefixed identifiers | method signatures with params | internal class references | specific return types
    31→
    32→Severity: HIGH. Flag each coupled AC with rewrite.
    33→
    34→- Coupled: `_install_des_module() copies src/des/`
    35→- Rewritten: `DES module importable from installation target after install`
    36→
    37→Rationale: AC = WHAT (observable behavior), never HOW (internal structure). Crafter decides during GREEN + REFACTOR.
    38→
    39→## Check 3: Step Decomposition Ratio
    40→
    41→Calculation: implementation steps / unique production files in `files_to_modify`
    42→
    43→Thresholds: acceptable <= 2.0 | warning 2.0-2.5 | reject > 2.5
    44→
    45→Identical pattern check: 3+ steps with identical AC differing by substitution -> require batching.
    46→
    47→## Check 4: Implementation Code in Roadmap
    48→
    49→Verify no implementation code. BLOCKER.
    50→
    51→Detection: code snippets/algorithms in descriptions | method implementations in AC | pseudocode/logic | variable names, loops, conditionals
    52→
    53→Architect defines WHAT; crafter decides HOW. Roadmap code prevents better solutions via TDD.
    54→
    55→## Check 5: Roadmap Concision and Precision
    56→
    57→Quantitative thresholds: total words 500 (1-3 steps) / 1500 (4-8) / 3000 (9-15) | step description max 50 words | AC max 5 per step, 30 words each | notes max 100 words
    58→
    59→Verbosity detection: multi-sentence blocks -> bullets | qualifiers (comprehensive, robust) -> delete | motivational language -> delete | redundancy -> state once | tutorials -> delete, assume expertise
    60→
    61→Precision: every AC single interpretation | testable outcome | business over technical jargon
    62→
    63→Severity: BLOCKER if word count exceeds threshold.
    64→
    65→## Check 6: Unit Test Boundary Validation
    66→
    67→Verify unit tests respect hexagonal boundaries.
    68→
    69→Rules: invoke through driving ports only | no domain entity unit tests | behavior focus | never import internal/private modules
    70→
    71→- Wrong: `Tests verify TemplateValidator._validate_schema() handles edge cases`
    72→- Right: `When invalid template provided to render_prompt(), ValidationError raised with clear message`
    73→
    74→Severity: HIGH.
    75→

1→---
     2→name: critique-dimensions
     3→description: Architecture quality critique dimensions for peer review. Load when invoking solution-architect-reviewer or performing self-review of architecture documents.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Architecture Quality Critique Dimensions
     7→
     8→## Dimension 1: Architectural Bias Detection
     9→
    10→### Technology Preference Bias
    11→Pattern: tech chosen by preference, not requirements. Detection: ADR lacks comparison matrix, choice not mapped to requirements, justified only as "best practice." Severity: HIGH.
    12→
    13→### Resume-Driven Development
    14→Pattern: complex/trendy tech without requirement justification. Examples: microservices for 3-person team, Kafka for 100 req/day, service mesh without complexity. Detection: complexity exceeds team size/requirements, tech adds resume value not solves problem. Severity: CRITICAL.
    15→
    16→### Latest Technology Bias
    17→Pattern: unproven tech (<6 months, small community) for production. Detection: check maturity, community, LTS, fallback plan. Severity: HIGH.
    18→
    19→## Dimension 2: ADR Quality Validation
    20→
    21→### Missing Context
    22→ADR lacks business problem, technical constraints, or quality attribute requirements. Future maintainers cannot validate. Severity: HIGH.
    23→
    24→### Missing Alternatives Analysis
    25→No alternatives (min 2 required). Each must be evaluated against requirements with rejection rationale. Severity: HIGH.
    26→
    27→### Missing Consequences
    28→Omits positive/negative consequences and trade-offs. Quality attribute impact not analyzed. Severity: MEDIUM.
    29→
    30→## Dimension 3: Completeness Validation
    31→
    32→### Missing Quality Attributes
    33→Architecture doesn't address required attributes. Verify: performance (latency, throughput) | scalability | security (auth, data protection) | maintainability (modularity, testability) | reliability (fault tolerance, recovery) | observability (logging, monitoring, alerting). Severity: CRITICAL.
    34→
    35→### Missing Performance Architecture
    36→Performance requirements exist but no optimization strategy (caching, indexing, rate limiting, CDN). Severity: CRITICAL.
    37→
    38→## Dimension 4: Implementation Feasibility
    39→
    40→### Team Capability Mismatch
    41→Requires expertise team lacks. Verify learning curve reasonable, training plan exists. Severity: HIGH.
    42→
    43→### Budget Constraints
    44→Infrastructure costs exceed budget. Verify cost estimate exists and aligns. Severity: HIGH.
    45→
    46→### Testability Validation
    47→Architecture prevents effective testing. Components must enable isolated testing with ports/adapters. Severity: CRITICAL.
    48→
    49→## Dimension 5: Priority Validation
    50→
    51→Validate roadmap addresses largest bottleneck.
    52→
    53→**Q1**: Largest bottleneck? (timing data must confirm primary problem)
    54→**Q2**: Simpler alternatives considered? (rejected alternatives required)
    55→**Q3**: Constraint prioritization correct? (quantified by impact, constraint-free first)
    56→**Q4**: Data-justified? (key decision with quantitative data)
    57→
    58→Failure: Q1=NO (wrong problem) | Q2=MISSING (no alternatives) | Q3=INVERTED (>50% solution for <30% problem) | Q4=NO_DATA for performance
    59→
    60→## Review Output Format
    61→
    62→```yaml
    63→review_id: "arch_rev_{timestamp}"
    64→reviewer: "solution-architect-reviewer"
    65→artifact: "docs/architecture/architecture.md, docs/adrs/*.md"
    66→iteration: {1 or 2}
    67→
    68→strengths:
    69→  - "{Positive decision with ADR reference}"
    70→
    71→issues_identified:
    72→  architectural_bias:
    73→    - issue: "{pattern detected}"
    74→      severity: "critical|high|medium|low"
    75→      location: "{ADR or section}"
    76→      recommendation: "{actionable fix}"
    77→  decision_quality:
    78→    - issue: "{ADR quality issue}"
    79→      severity: "high"
    80→      location: "ADR-{number}"
    81→      recommendation: "{add missing section}"
    82→  completeness_gaps:
    83→    - issue: "{quality attribute not addressed}"
    84→      severity: "critical"
    85→      recommendation: "{add architecture section}"
    86→  implementation_feasibility:
    87→    - issue: "{capability, budget, testability concern}"
    88→      severity: "high"
    89→      recommendation: "{simplify or add mitigation}"
    90→  priority_validation:
    91→    q1_largest_bottleneck:
    92→      evidence: "{data or NOT PROVIDED}"
    93→      assessment: "YES|NO|UNCLEAR"
    94→    q2_simple_alternatives:
    95→      assessment: "ADEQUATE|INADEQUATE|MISSING"
    96→    q3_constraint_prioritization:
    97→      assessment: "CORRECT|INVERTED|NOT_ANALYZED"
    98→    q4_data_justified:
    99→      assessment: "JUSTIFIED|UNJUSTIFIED|NO_DATA"
   100→
   101→approval_status: "approved|rejected_pending_revisions|conditionally_approved"
   102→critical_issues_count: {number}
   103→high_issues_count: {number}
   104→```
   105→
   106→## Severity Classification
   107→
   108→- **Critical**: resume-driven dev, missing critical quality attributes, untestable, wrong problem
   109→- **High**: technology bias, incomplete ADRs, feasibility concerns, missing data
   110→- **Medium**: missing consequences, minor completeness gaps
   111→- **Low**: documentation improvements, naming consistency
   112→

1→# Unified Identity Hub-and-Spoke: Architecture Design
     2→
     3→## System Context and Capabilities
     4→
     5→The unified identity feature introduces a hub-and-spoke identity model where both humans and agents are first-class citizens in the knowledge graph. This enables:
     6→
     7→- **Unified audit trail**: Every graph mutation is attributed to an identity (human or agent)
     8→- **Dual-label attribution**: Agent actions show both the agent actor and the accountable human via managed_by chain
     9→- **Scoped authorization**: Per-identity permission overrides on top of role-based defaults
    10→- **Agent mention resolution**: Extraction pipeline recognizes agent references in conversation
    11→
    12→## C4 System Context (L1)
    13→
    14→```mermaid
    15→C4Context
    16→  title System Context: Brain Platform with Unified Identity
    17→
    18→  Person(marcus, "Workspace Owner", "Authenticates via GitHub OAuth, manages workspace")
    19→  System(brain, "Brain Platform", "AI-native business management with knowledge graph")
    20→
    21→  System_Ext(github, "GitHub", "OAuth provider, code hosting")
    22→  System_Ext(openai, "OpenAI API", "Embeddings (text-embedding-3-small)")
    23→  System_Ext(llm, "LLM Provider", "Chat completion (Sonnet), extraction (Haiku)")
    24→
    25→  Boundary(agents, "AI Agents") {
    26→    System_Ext(code_agent, "Code Agent", "Connects via MCP protocol")
    27→  }
    28→
    29→  Rel(marcus, brain, "Chats, creates workspaces, reviews agent suggestions")
    30→  Rel(code_agent, brain, "Authenticates as agent identity, writes decisions/tasks")
    31→  Rel(brain, github, "OAuth login, PR/commit data")
    32→  Rel(brain, openai, "Generates embeddings for identities and entities")
    33→  Rel(brain, llm, "Chat responses, entity extraction")
    34→```
    35→
    36→## C4 Container (L2)
    37→
    38→```mermaid
    39→C4Container
    40→  title Container Diagram: Unified Identity Integration
    41→
    42→  Person(user, "Workspace Owner")
    43→  System_Ext(mcp_client, "MCP Agent")
    44→
    45→  Container_Boundary(server, "Bun Server") {
    46→    Container(auth, "Auth Module", "better-auth + adapter", "OAuth login, session management, identity_id on session/account")
    47→    Container(iam, "IAM Module", "identity.ts + authority.ts", "Resolves email/provider to identity, checks authority with overrides")
    48→    Container(chat, "Chat Pipeline", "ingress + processor + handler", "Builds identity-based execution context, dispatches to agents")
    49→    Container(extraction, "Extraction Pipeline", "Haiku LLM + resolution", "Extracts entities, resolves assignees to identity records")
    50→    Container(workspace, "Workspace Module", "routes + bootstrap", "Creates workspaces, bootstraps identity wrapping and agent registration")
    51→    Container(audit, "Audit Queries", "graph queries", "Dual-label attribution, agent suggestion tracking")
    52→    Container(mcp_auth, "MCP Auth", "token validation", "Resolves MCP tokens to agent identity")
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB", "v3.0 SCHEMAFULL", "identity hub, person/agent spokes, graph relations, vector indexes")
    56→
    57→  Rel(user, auth, "Authenticates via GitHub OAuth")
    58→  Rel(mcp_client, mcp_auth, "Authenticates with OAuth token")
    59→  Rel(auth, iam, "Resolves session to identity")
    60→  Rel(mcp_auth, iam, "Resolves token to agent identity")
    61→  Rel(iam, surreal, "Queries person->identity spoke traversal")
    62→  Rel(chat, iam, "Gets identity from session")
    63→  Rel(chat, extraction, "Triggers entity extraction")
    64→  Rel(chat, audit, "Queries entity detail with attribution")
    65→  Rel(extraction, iam, "Resolves assignee names to identity")
    66→  Rel(workspace, surreal, "Creates identity + agent + spoke edges")
    67→  Rel(audit, surreal, "Traverses managed_by chain for dual-label")
    68→  Rel(auth, surreal, "Reads/writes session with identity_id")
    69→```
    70→
    71→## C4 Component (L3) -- Identity Resolution Subsystem
    72→
    73→The identity resolution path is the most complex subsystem due to the spoke traversal, so it warrants L3 detail.
    74→
    75→```mermaid
    76→C4Component
    77→  title Component Diagram: Identity Resolution
    78→
    79→  Container_Boundary(iam_boundary, "IAM Module") {
    80→    Component(resolve_email, "resolveByEmail", "Function", "Finds person by email, traverses spoke to identity")
    81→    Component(resolve_identity, "resolveIdentity", "Function", "Finds person by provider/id, traverses spoke to identity")
    82→    Component(resolve_workspace_identity, "resolveWorkspaceIdentity", "Function", "Finds identity by name in workspace (humans + agents)")
    83→    Component(check_authority, "checkAuthority", "Function", "Override edge -> role default -> agent_type default -> blocked")
    84→  }
    85→
    86→  Container_Boundary(auth_boundary, "Auth Module") {
    87→    Component(better_auth, "better-auth config", "Config", "Maps user->person, session.userId->identity_id")
    88→    Component(adapter, "SurrealDB adapter", "Adapter", "FK map: identity_id -> identity table")
    89→    Component(oauth_claims, "customAccessTokenClaims", "Function", "Resolves person->identity for JWT workspace claims")
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  Container_Boundary(chat_boundary, "Chat Pipeline") {
    93→    Component(ingress, "chat-ingress", "Handler", "Extracts identity_id from session, builds context")
    94→    Component(context, "ChatToolExecutionContext", "Type", "identityRecord replaces personRecord")
    95→  }
    96→
    97→  ContainerDb(surreal, "SurrealDB")
    98→
    99→  Rel(better_auth, adapter, "Uses for CRUD")
   100→  Rel(adapter, surreal, "Reads/writes session, account with identity_id FK")
   101→  Rel(better_auth, oauth_claims, "Calls on token issue")
   102→  Rel(oauth_claims, resolve_email, "Resolves user to identity for claims")
   103→  Rel(ingress, resolve_email, "Session user email -> identity")
   104→  Rel(ingress, context, "Populates identityRecord")
   105→  Rel(resolve_email, surreal, "SELECT person WHERE email -> <-identity_person<-identity")
   106→  Rel(resolve_identity, surreal, "SELECT person WHERE provider match -> <-identity_person<-identity")
   107→  Rel(resolve_workspace_identity, surreal, "SELECT identity WHERE name match AND workspace")
   108→  Rel(check_authority, surreal, "SELECT authorized_to, authority_scope")
   109→```
   110→
   111→## Architecture Decisions
   112→
   113→### Decision: Sequential story implementation (not parallel)
   114→
   115→Stories US-UI-001 through US-UI-005 have strict sequential dependencies. The schema must exist (001) before identities can be created (002), identities must exist before edges migrate (003), edges must migrate before auth rewires (004), and all must be complete before audit queries work (005). This is inherently serial.
   116→
   117→US-UI-006 (authorization) can begin after US-UI-002 but should follow US-UI-005 for integration simplicity.
   118→US-UI-007 (extraction) depends on US-UI-003 and can follow US-UI-005.
   119→
   120→### Decision: Identity bootstrap in workspace creation transaction
   121→
   122→The identity wrapping and agent registration (US-UI-002) runs inside the workspace creation transaction. This ensures atomicity: a workspace always has at least one identity (the owner) and its template agent identities.
   123→
   124→For existing workspaces, the bootstrap is idempotent and can run on next workspace load.
   125→
   126→### Decision: better-auth user model stays mapped to person
   127→
   128→The better-auth `user` model continues mapping to the `person` table. Email-based signup creates `person` records. The identity wrapping happens as a post-creation hook: after person is created, the bootstrap creates the identity hub and spoke edge.
   129→
   130→This avoids modifying better-auth's internal user model, which assumes email/password fields on the user table.
   131→
   132→### Decision: ChatToolExecutionContext carries identityRecord, not personRecord
   133→
   134→The primary actor reference in tool execution context changes from `personRecord: RecordId<"person">` to `identityRecord: RecordId<"identity">`. This is a clean break (not an addition) because:
   135→
   136→- All downstream consumers need identity, not person
   137→- Carrying both creates ambiguity about which to use
   138→- The person spoke can always be reached via graph traversal from identity when needed
   139→
   140→### Decision: Extraction resolution searches identity table first
   141→
   142→Post-migration, `resolveWorkspaceIdentity()` searches `identity.name` in the workspace directly, rather than searching person then traversing to identity. This:
   143→
   144→- Handles both human and agent name resolution in one path
   145→- Eliminates the extra spoke traversal for the common case
   146→- Falls back to email-based person lookup only when direct name match fails
   147→
   148→## Quality Attribute Strategies
   149→
   150→### Auditability (primary driver)
   151→
   152→- Every ownership field references `identity`, enabling single-table audit queries
   153→- `managed_by` chain provides human accountability for all agent actions
   154→- Dual-label format: actor identity + accountable human in query results
   155→
   156→### Maintainability
   157→
   158→- Hub-spoke pattern isolates type-specific fields in spoke tables
   159→- New actor types (e.g., "system" for cron jobs) require only: new spoke table + spoke edge, no ownership field changes
   160→- Authority system extensible via relation edges, no code changes for new permissions
   161→
   162→### Security
   163→
   164→- `humanPresent` flag derived from `identity.type = 'human'`, not assumed from session existence
   165→- Authority override edges are workspace-scoped
   166→- Fail-safe: no authority_scope match = blocked
   167→
   168→### Performance
   169→
   170→- Identity resolution adds 1 spoke traversal hop for email-based auth (negligible for auth-frequency operations)
   171→- Direct identity.name search avoids the extra hop for extraction resolution
   172→- HNSW index on identity.embedding enables vector search without additional infrastructure
   173→
   174→## Deployment Architecture
   175→
   176→No changes to deployment. The feature is entirely schema + application code:
   177→
   178→1. Schema migration applied via `bun migrate` (creates tables, modifies fields, updates functions)
   179→2. Application restart picks up new TypeScript types and resolution logic
   180→3. Workspace bootstrap creates identity records on first load
   181→

   277→### Roadmap Efficiency Check
   278→

   282→- No identical-pattern steps detected
   283→
   284→## Rejected Simpler Alternatives
   285→
   286→### Alternative 1: Add agent_type field to person table
   287→
   288→- **What**: Add `type: 'human' | 'agent'`, `agent_type`, `managed_by` directly to person table.
   289→- **Expected impact**: 70% -- enables agent ownership but pollutes person semantics.
   290→- **Why insufficient**: `person` has UNIQUE email index, email-based resolution, and better-auth user model mapping. Agents don't have emails. Would require making contact_email optional (breaking existing constraints) and adding conditional logic everywhere.
   291→
   292→### Alternative 2: Use agent_session as the agent identity
   293→
   294→- **What**: Promote existing `agent_session` records to serve as identity references.
   295→- **Expected impact**: 40% -- agent_session is per-session, not per-agent. Multiple sessions per agent type.
   296→- **Why insufficient**: An agent's identity persists across sessions. `agent_session` represents a single work session, not an enduring identity. Ownership should survive session termination.
   297→
   298→### Why the hub-spoke solution is necessary
   299→
   300→1. Simple alternatives fail because SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL cannot enforce conditional field requirements on a single table, and the person table has semantic/structural constraints (UNIQUE email, better-auth model mapping) incompatible with agent records.
   301→2. Complexity justified by: single traversal point for all attribution queries, clean type-specific schema enforcement via spoke tables, and extensibility for future actor types without ownership field changes.
   302→

1→# Unified Identity: Data Models
     2→
     3→## New Tables
     4→
     5→### identity (hub)
     6→
     7→| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
     8→|-------|------|----------|-------|
     9→| name | string | yes | Display name |
    10→| type | string | yes | ASSERT IN ['human', 'agent', 'system'] |
    11→| role | option\<string\> | no | Functional role: owner, management, coder, observer, etc. |
    12→| workspace | record\<workspace\> | yes | Scoped to workspace |
    13→| embedding | option\<array\<float\>\> | no | HNSW 1536-dim for vector search |
    14→| created_at | datetime | yes | |
    15→
    16→**Indexes**:
    17→- `identity_workspace` on `workspace`
    18→- `identity_type_workspace` on `type, workspace`
    19→- `idx_identity_embedding` HNSW DIMENSION 1536 DIST COSINE on `embedding`
    20→
    21→### agent (spoke)
    22→
    23→| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
    24→|-------|------|----------|-------|
    25→| agent_type | string | yes | Matches authority_scope enum: code_agent, architect, management, design_partner, observer |
    26→| model | option\<string\> | no | LLM model identifier |
    27→| managed_by | record\<identity\> | yes | Human identity responsible for this agent |
    28→| created_at | datetime | yes | |
    29→
    30→### identity_person (spoke edge)
    31→
    32→| Constraint | Value |
    33→|------------|-------|
    34→| TYPE RELATION | IN identity OUT person |
    35→| added_at | datetime |
    36→
    37→### identity_agent (spoke edge)
    38→
    39→| Constraint | Value |
    40→|------------|-------|
    41→| TYPE RELATION | IN identity OUT agent |
    42→| added_at | datetime |
    43→
    44→## Modified Tables
    45→
    46→### Fields changing from record\<person\> to record\<identity\>
    47→
    48→| Table | Field | Before | After |
    49→|-------|-------|--------|-------|
    50→| task | owner | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    51→| feature | owner | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    52→| decision | decided_by | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    53→| decision | confirmed_by | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    54→| question | assigned_to | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    55→| observation | resolved_by | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    56→| git_commit | author | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    57→| pull_request | author | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    58→| session | person_id | record\<person\> | REMOVE; add identity_id record\<identity\> |
    59→| account | person_id | record\<person\> | REMOVE; add identity_id record\<identity\> |
    60→
    61→### Relation tables changing IN constraint
    62→
    63→| Table | Before | After |
    64→|-------|--------|-------|
    65→| owns | IN person OUT task \| project \| feature | IN identity OUT task \| project \| feature |
    66→| member_of | IN person OUT workspace | IN identity OUT workspace |
    67→| attended_by | IN meeting OUT person | IN meeting OUT identity |
    68→
    69→### person table modifications
    70→
    71→| Change | Detail |
    72→|--------|--------|
    73→| REMOVE FIELD | `identities` (redundant with account table) |
    74→| No other changes | person retains name, role, contact_email, contact_phone, email_verified, image, embedding, created_at, updated_at |
    75→
    76→### OAuth tables (better-auth managed)
    77→
    78→| Table | Field | Before | After |
    79→|-------|-------|--------|-------|
    80→| oauthClient | userId | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    81→| oauthAccessToken | userId | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    82→| oauthRefreshToken | userId | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |
    83→| oauthConsent | userId | option\<record\<person\>\> | option\<record\<identity\>\> |

    85→## US-UI-006: Authority Model Extensions
    86→
    87→### authority_scope modifications
    88→
    89→| Change | Detail |
    90→|--------|--------|
    91→| Add field | `role` option\<string\> -- matches identity.role for role-based lookup |
    92→| Retain | `agent_type` field for backwards compatibility during transition |
    93→| New index | `authority_scope_role_lookup` on role, action, workspace UNIQUE |
    94→
    95→### authorized_to (new relation -- per-identity override)
    96→
    97→| Constraint | Value |
    98→|------------|-------|
    99→| TYPE RELATION | IN identity OUT authority_scope |
   100→| permission | string ASSERT IN ['auto', 'provisional', 'propose', 'blocked'] |
   101→| created_at | datetime |
   102→
   103→## Graph Traversal Patterns
   104→
   105→### Identity resolution (email -> identity)
   106→
   107→```
   108→person(contact_email) -> <-identity_person<-identity
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Managed-by chain (agent -> accountable human)
   112→
   113→```
   114→identity(type=agent) -> identity_agent -> agent.managed_by -> identity(type=human)
   115→```
   116→
   117→### Dual-label audit (who did this + who is accountable)
   118→
   119→```
   120→task.owner -> identity {
   121→  type = 'human': self is accountable
   122→  type = 'agent': ->identity_agent->agent.managed_by -> identity(type=human)
   123→}
   124→```
   125→
   126→## Extraction Pipeline Impact
   127→
   128→### Current: person resolution
   129→
   130→```
   131→extraction schema -> assignee_name -> resolveWorkspacePerson() -> RecordId<"person">
   132→```
   133→
   134→### After: identity resolution
   135→
   136→```
   137→extraction schema -> assignee_name -> resolveWorkspaceIdentity() -> RecordId<"identity">
   138→```
   139→
   140→Resolution order: exact name match on identity.name (workspace-scoped) -> email match via person spoke -> undefined.
   141→
   142→### US-UI-007: Agent mention resolution (COULD)
   143→
   144→```
   145→extraction schema -> assignee_name -> resolveWorkspaceIdentity() {
   146→  1. match identity WHERE type = 'human' AND name match
   147→  2. match identity WHERE type = 'agent' AND name match
   148→  3. match identity WHERE type = 'agent' AND ->identity_agent->agent.agent_type match
   149→  4. email fallback via person spoke
   150→}
   151→```
   152→

1→# Unified Identity: Component Boundaries
     2→
     3→## Affected Components
     4→
     5→### 1. Schema Layer (SurrealDB migrations)
     6→
     7→**Responsibility**: Define identity hub, agent spoke, spoke edges, and migrate all record<person> fields.
     8→
     9→**Boundary**: Migration scripts in `schema/migrations/`. Applied via `bun migrate`. No TypeScript logic.
    10→
    11→**Stories**: US-UI-001 (hub schema), US-UI-003 (edge migration), US-UI-004 (auth fields), US-UI-006 (authority extensions)
    12→
    13→**Integration points**:
    14→- Consumed by: every component that reads/writes ownership fields
    15→- Migration sequence number: 0017+ (after existing 0016)
    16→
    17→---
    18→
    19→### 2. Identity Bootstrap (workspace creation flow)
    20→
    21→**Responsibility**: Wrap person records in identity hubs, register template agent identities, create spoke edges. Runs during workspace creation and as idempotent bootstrap.
    22→
    23→**Boundary**: New module in `app/src/server/workspace/` or dedicated `app/src/server/identity/` directory. Called from workspace creation handler.
    24→
    25→**Stories**: US-UI-002
    26→
    27→**Integration points**:
    28→- Depends on: workspace creation handler (`workspace-routes.ts`), person records, identity schema
    29→- Produces: identity records, agent records, identity_person edges, identity_agent edges, member_of edges (now IN identity)
    30→- Triggered by: workspace creation (automatic), manual bootstrap endpoint (idempotent)

    32→---
    33→
    34→### 3. IAM / Identity Resolution
    35→
    36→**Responsibility**: Resolve authenticated users and agents to identity records. Replace person-based resolution with identity-based resolution.
    37→
    38→**Boundary**: `app/src/server/iam/identity.ts` (existing file, modified)

    40→**Stories**: US-UI-004

    43→- `resolveIdentity()`: provider+providerId -> RecordId<"identity"> (was RecordId<"person">)
    44→- `resolveByEmail()`: email -> RecordId<"identity"> (was RecordId<"person">)
    45→- Resolution path: email -> person -> <-identity_person<-identity (one extra hop)

    50→### 4. Auth Adapter and Config
    51→
    52→**Responsibility**: Bridge better-auth session/account models to identity-based references.

    56→**Stories**: US-UI-004

    60→- `session.userId` field maps to `identity_id` (was `person_id`)
    61→- `account.userId` field maps to `identity_id` (was `person_id`)
    62→- OAuth token claims: `customAccessTokenClaims` must resolve person -> identity for workspace membership query

    77→**Integration points**:
    78→- `chat-ingress.ts`: session -> identity_id -> identityRecord (was personRecord)
    79→- `chat-processor.ts`: passes identityRecord through pipeline
    80→- `handler.ts`: receives identityRecord, passes to tool context
    81→- `tools/types.ts`: type definition change
    82→- `workspaceOwnerRecord`: also changes from RecordId<"person"> to RecordId<"identity">
    83→- `humanPresent` flag: derived from identity.type = 'human' (was implicit from session)

   147→### 10. Graph Functions (SurrealQL)

1→# Unified Identity: Technology Stack
     2→
     3→## No New Dependencies
     4→
     5→This feature is implemented entirely within the existing technology stack. No new libraries, frameworks, or services are introduced.
     6→
     7→| Layer | Technology | Version | License | Role in Feature |
     8→|-------|-----------|---------|---------|-----------------|
     9→| Database | SurrealDB | v3.0 | BSL 1.1 (existing) | Hub-spoke schema, migrations, graph traversals |
    10→| Runtime | Bun | latest (existing) | MIT | Server runtime, migration runner |
    11→| Language | TypeScript | 5.x (existing) | Apache 2.0 | Type changes, identity resolution logic |
    12→| Auth | better-auth | latest (existing) | MIT | Session/account field mapping updates |
    13→| SDK | surrealdb (JS) | v2 (existing) | Apache 2.0 | RecordId type changes, queries |
    14→| AI SDK | Vercel AI SDK | latest (existing) | Apache 2.0 | Tool context type propagation |
    15→| Embedding | OpenAI text-embedding-3-small | (existing) | Proprietary API | Identity embeddings (same pipeline) |
    16→
    17→## Technology Decisions
    18→
    19→### Why no dedicated identity service
    20→
    21→**Decision**: Identity resolution remains an in-process module, not a separate service.
    22→
    23→**Rationale**:
    24→- Team size: 1 developer + AI agents. Operational overhead of a separate service is unjustified.
    25→- Identity resolution is 1-2 SurrealDB queries. No computational complexity requiring isolation.
    26→- All callers are in the same Bun process (chat handler, MCP auth, extraction pipeline).
    27→- SurrealDB graph traversals (spoke edges) are native -- no need for caching layer.
    28→
    29→### Why SurrealDB TYPE RELATION for spoke edges (not record links)
    30→
    31→**Decision**: Use `identity_person` and `identity_agent` as TYPE RELATION tables, not embedded `record<person>` fields on identity.
    32→
    33→**Rationale**:
    34→- TYPE RELATION enables bidirectional graph traversal (`<-identity_person<-identity` from person side).
    35→- Metadata on the edge (added_at) follows existing relation patterns (owns, member_of).
    36→- SurrealDB indexes on relation `in`/`out` fields enable efficient spoke lookups.
    37→- Embedded record links would require scanning identity table to find "which identity wraps this person."
    38→
    39→### Why HNSW index on identity.embedding
    40→
    41→**Decision**: Add HNSW vector index on identity table for semantic search.
    42→
    43→**Rationale**:
    44→- Chat agent's `search_entities` tool should find identities by semantic similarity (e.g., "who manages the PM agent?").
    45→- Same 1536-dimension embedding model already used for all other entity tables.
    46→- KNN+WHERE split pattern required (per documented SurrealDB v3.0 bug) since identity will have a workspace index.
    47→
    48→### Why breaking schema change (no data migration)
    49→
    50→**Decision**: Per project convention, schema changes are breaking. No backfill scripts.
    51→
    52→**Rationale**:
    53→- AGENTS.md: "This project does NOT maintain backwards compatibility with existing data."
    54→- Identity bootstrap runs on workspace creation -- fresh data on next setup.
    55→- Eliminates migration script complexity and rollback risk.
    56→
    57→## SurrealDB Constraints and Workarounds
    58→
    59→| Constraint | Impact | Workaround |
    60→|-----------|--------|------------|
    61→| KNN + WHERE bug | Identity vector search with workspace filter returns empty | Split into LET + filter (existing pattern) |
    62→| SCHEMAFULL conditional fields | Cannot enforce "agent_type required when type=agent" on single table | Hub-spoke: agent-specific fields on separate `agent` table with its own SCHEMAFULL |
    63→| DEFINE ANALYZER outside transaction | If identity gets fulltext index, analyzer must precede BEGIN TRANSACTION | Place DEFINE ANALYZER before transaction block in migration |
    64→| No ALTER TABLE ADD FIELD | Adding identity_id to session | Use DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE |
    65→| search::score in functions | If identity gets fulltext search | Run from app layer (existing pattern) |
    66→

1→# ADR-010: Unified Identity Hub-and-Spoke Model
     2→
     3→## Status
     4→
     5→Proposed
     6→
     7→## Context
     8→
     9→The system currently uses `person` records as the sole identity primitive. Agents operate through `agent_session` records with bare string identifiers (`agent` field). This creates several problems:
    10→
    11→1. **Attribution gap**: Agent actions are invisible or falsely attributed to humans. The `owns` relation is `IN person OUT task|project|feature` -- agents cannot own anything.
    12→2. **Audit trail blind spot**: Cannot answer "who did this?" for agent actions. `suggestion.suggested_by` and `observation.source_agent` are bare strings with no graph identity.
    13→3. **Authorization fragility**: `authority_scope` uses `agent_type` string matching, making per-instance permission overrides impossible.
    14→4. **Session type mismatch**: `session.person_id` and `account.person_id` assume all authenticated actors are humans.
    15→
    16→The system has 10+ tables with `record<person>` ownership fields and 2 relation tables (`owns`, `member_of`) constrained to `IN person`.
    17→
    18→## Decision
    19→
    20→Introduce a hub-and-spoke identity model:
    21→
    22→- **Hub**: New `identity` table with shared fields (name, type, role, workspace, embedding). Type enum: `['human', 'agent', 'system']`.
    23→- **Spokes**: `person` (human-specific: email, image) and new `agent` table (agent-specific: agent_type, model, managed_by).
    24→- **Spoke edges**: `identity_person` (TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT person) and `identity_agent` (TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT agent).
    25→- **Edge migration**: All `record<person>` ownership fields and relation constraints migrate to `record<identity>`.
    26→- **Auth rewiring**: `session.person_id` and `account.person_id` become `session.identity_id` and `account.identity_id`.
    27→
    28→The `person` table remains as a spoke for human-specific data. No data migration (project convention: breaking schema changes).
    29→
    30→## Alternatives Considered
    31→
    32→### Alternative 1: Extend person table with agent fields
    33→
    34→Add `type`, `agent_type`, `model` fields directly to `person`. Rename table conceptually as "actor."
    35→
    36→- **Pros**: Zero new tables, minimal migration surface, no spoke edges needed.
    37→- **Cons**: Semantic mismatch (`person` table holding agent data), human-specific fields (email, image) become optional noise on agent records, no clean separation of concerns, `person_email` UNIQUE index conflicts with agents that have no email.
    38→- **Rejected because**: Violates single-responsibility at the schema level. The `person` table has a UNIQUE email index and email-based resolution that would need complex conditional logic for agents.
    39→
    40→### Alternative 2: Polymorphic actor with discriminated union
    41→
    42→Create a single `actor` table with all fields, using `type` discriminator. No separate spoke tables.
    43→
    44→- **Pros**: Single table, simple queries, no joins/traversals for identity.
    45→- **Cons**: Wide table with many NONE fields per record type, no schema enforcement of type-specific required fields (SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL cannot conditional-require by discriminator), extraction pipeline's person resolution would need rewriting.
    46→- **Rejected because**: SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL mode cannot enforce "agent_type is required when type = 'agent'" -- the schema would be permissive where it should be strict.
    47→
    48→### Alternative 3: Keep person-only, add agent_identity as separate parallel
    49→
    50→Keep `person` for humans, add `agent_identity` for agents, use `record<person|agent_identity>` union types in ownership fields.
    51→
    52→- **Pros**: No hub table, simpler graph.
    53→- **Cons**: Every ownership field becomes a union type (`record<person|agent_identity>`), every query needs to handle both types, no single traversal path for "who did this?", graph functions would need dual-path logic.
    54→- **Rejected because**: Union-typed ownership fields create query complexity that scales with every new actor type. Hub-and-spoke centralizes the polymorphism.
    55→
    56→## Consequences
    57→
    58→### Positive
    59→
    60→- Single `identity` node for all attribution queries -- "who did this?" resolves to one table.
    61→- `managed_by` chain enables dual-label audit trails (agent actor + accountable human).
    62→- Per-identity authorization overrides become possible via `authorized_to` edges.
    63→- `person` table retains its existing structure and UNIQUE email index.
    64→- Agent identities are first-class graph citizens: can own tasks, make decisions, have embeddings for vector search.
    65→
    66→### Negative
    67→
    68→- All `record<person>` ownership fields across 5+ tables must migrate to `record<identity>`.
    69→- Auth resolution adds one hop: email -> person -> identity (spoke traversal).
    70→- `ChatToolExecutionContext.personRecord` becomes `identityRecord` -- affects chat handler, ingress, processor, MCP auth, and all tool implementations.
    71→- better-auth adapter maps `user` model to `person` -- the adapter FK map must also handle the identity indirection for session/account tables.
    72→- Graph functions (`fn::workspace_entity_ids`, `fn::entity_edges`, etc.) that reference `person` in traversals or unions need updating.
    73→
    74→### Quality Attribute Impact
    75→
    76→| Attribute | Impact | Direction |
    77→|-----------|--------|-----------|
    78→| Auditability | Unified attribution for all actors | Positive |
    79→| Maintainability | Hub-spoke isolates type-specific fields, extensible for new actor types | Positive |
    80→| Security | Explicit identity type check replaces implicit session assumption | Positive |
    81→| Performance | +1 spoke traversal hop on email-based auth (auth-frequency, negligible) | Neutral |
    82→| Testability | Identity resolution is pure function (email -> RecordId), mockable at port boundary | Positive |
    83→| Observability | Spoke traversal failures must be logged; identity resolution errors surface clearly | Requires attention |
    84→
    85→### Observability Note
    86→
    87→Identity resolution errors (spoke traversal returning no identity for a valid person) must fail fast with structured logging. The resolution path email -> person -> identity involves one extra query; if the spoke edge is missing, the system should throw (not silently degrade) per project failure handling conventions.
    88→
    89→### Migration Surface (Quantified)
    90→
    91→| Category | Count | Files/Tables |
    92→|----------|-------|--------------|
    93→| Schema fields changing `record<person>` to `record<identity>` | 8 | task.owner, feature.owner, decision.decided_by, decision.confirmed_by, question.assigned_to, observation.resolved_by, git_commit.author, pull_request.author |
    94→| Relation tables changing IN constraint | 2 | owns (IN person -> IN identity), member_of (IN person -> IN identity) |
    95→| Session/Account fields renaming | 2 | session.person_id -> identity_id, account.person_id -> identity_id |
    96→| TypeScript files with `RecordId<"person">` | 10 | chat/handler, chat-ingress, chat-processor, tools/types, mcp/types, mcp/auth, iam/identity, extraction/person, graph/queries, observation/queries |
    97→| OAuth tables with `record<person>` | 4 | oauthClient.userId, oauthAccessToken.userId, oauthRefreshToken.userId, oauthConsent.userId |
    98→| New tables | 3 | identity, agent, identity_person, identity_agent |
    99→

1→# Unified Identity Node -- User Stories
     2→
     3→## Story Map
     4→
     5→```
     6→Job 1: Unified Audit Trail (MUST)
     7→  |
     8→  +-- US-UI-001: Identity Hub Schema (foundation)
     9→  +-- US-UI-002: Identity Wrapping (person + agent creation)
    10→  +-- US-UI-003: Edge Migration (repoint all ownership)
    11→  +-- US-UI-004: Auth Rewiring (session + account)
    12→  +-- US-UI-005: Dual-Label Audit Trail (the payoff query)
    13→  |
    14→Job 2: Scoped Authorization (SHOULD)
    15→  |
    16→  +-- US-UI-006: Role-Based Authority with Overrides
    17→  |
    18→Job 4: Full-Provenance Extraction (COULD)
    19→  |
    20→  +-- US-UI-007: Agent Mention Resolution in Extraction Pipeline
    21→```
    22→
    23→Row 1 (MVP): US-UI-001 through US-UI-005 (must-have, sequential dependency)
    24→Row 2: US-UI-006 (should-have, depends on US-UI-002)
    25→Row 3: US-UI-007 (could-have, depends on US-UI-002 + US-UI-003)
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→# US-UI-001: Identity Hub and Spoke Schema
    30→
    31→## Problem
    32→Marcus is a workspace owner running an AI-native business management platform where agents (PM Agent, Code Agent) perform actions alongside humans. He cannot answer "who did this?" because agents have no first-class identity -- `agent_session` uses bare strings, and all ownership edges point at `person` records only. Agent actions are either invisible or falsely attributed.
    33→
    34→## Who
    35→- Workspace owner | Brownfield system with existing person + agent_session tables | Needs unified identity model before any downstream work is possible
    36→
    37→## Solution
    38→Create the `identity` hub table, `agent` spoke table, and spoke relation edge tables (`identity_person`, `identity_agent`) via a SurrealDB schema migration. The `identity` table holds shared fields (name, type, role, embedding, workspace). The `person` table remains as-is for human-specific fields. The `agent` table holds agent-specific fields (agent_type, model, managed_by).
    39→
    40→## Domain Examples
    41→
    42→### 1: Human Identity Structure -- Marcus Oliveira
    43→Marcus Oliveira is the workspace owner. His `identity` record has `name: "Marcus Oliveira"`, `type: "human"`, `role: "owner"`, `workspace: workspace:san-jose`. An `identity_person` edge links to `person:marcus` which retains `contact_email: "marcus@conductor.dev"` and `image`. OAuth provider data lives in the `account` table (linked via `identity_id`).
    44→
    45→### 2: Agent Identity Structure -- PM Agent
    46→The PM Agent for workspace san-jose gets `identity` record with `name: "PM Agent"`, `type: "agent"`, `role: "management"`, `workspace: workspace:san-jose`. An `identity_agent` edge links to `agent:pm-ws-san-jose` which has `agent_type: "management"`, `model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"`, `managed_by: identity:marcus-human`.
    47→
    48→### 3: Invalid Type Rejection -- Schema Enforcement
    49→A developer accidentally tries to create an identity with `type: "bot"`. The SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL assertion rejects the record with a validation error, enforcing the `['human', 'agent', 'system']` enum constraint at the database level.
    50→
    51→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    52→
    53→### Scenario: Human identity hub record created successfully
    54→Given workspace "san-jose" exists
    55→When an identity record is created with name "Marcus Oliveira", type "human", role "owner", workspace "san-jose"
    56→Then the identity record is persisted with all fields
    57→And a created_at timestamp is set automatically
    58→
    59→### Scenario: Agent identity hub record created successfully
    60→Given workspace "san-jose" exists
    61→When an identity record is created with name "PM Agent", type "agent", role "management", workspace "san-jose"
    62→Then the identity record is persisted with all fields
    63→And the type field is "agent"
    64→
    65→### Scenario: Agent spoke table enforces managed_by
    66→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human"
    67→When an agent spoke record is created with agent_type "management", model "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", managed_by identity "Marcus Oliveira"
    68→Then the agent spoke record is persisted
    69→And managed_by references a valid identity record
    70→
    71→### Scenario: Spoke relation edges connect hub to spoke
    72→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists and person "Marcus Oliveira" exists
    73→When an identity_person relation edge is created from identity to person
    74→Then the edge is persisted as TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT person
    75→And traversal from identity via ->identity_person->person returns the person record
    76→
    77→### Scenario: Invalid identity type rejected by schema
    78→Given workspace "san-jose" exists
    79→When an identity record is created with type "bot"
    80→Then the creation fails with a schema validation error
    81→And types "human", "agent", and "system" are all accepted
    82→
    83→## Acceptance Criteria
    84→- [ ] `identity` table is SCHEMAFULL with fields: name (string), type (string, ASSERT IN ['human', 'agent', 'system']), role (option<string>), embedding (option<array<float>>), workspace (record<workspace>), created_at (datetime)
    85→- [ ] `agent` table is SCHEMAFULL with fields: agent_type (string), model (option<string>), managed_by (record<identity>), created_at (datetime)
    86→- [ ] `identity_person` is TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT person
    87→- [ ] `identity_agent` is TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT agent
    88→- [ ] HNSW index defined on identity.embedding for vector search
    89→- [ ] `person.identities` field removed (redundant with `account` table)
    90→- [ ] Invalid type values are rejected at schema level
    91→
    92→## Technical Notes
    93→- Migration file: `schema/migrations/00XX_unified_identity.surql` (determine next sequence number from existing migrations)
    94→- Apply via `bun migrate` per project convention
    95→- Wrap in `BEGIN TRANSACTION; ... COMMIT TRANSACTION;`
    96→- `person.identities` field (array of OAuth provider/id pairs) is removed in this migration — it is redundant with the `account` table which already stores `provider_id`, `account_id`, and OAuth tokens per provider. Use `REMOVE FIELD identities ON person;` in the migration.
    97→- No other `person` table fields are modified in this story -- ownership edge changes happen in US-UI-003
    98→- The `type` enum includes `'system'` for automated system-level actions (e.g., scheduled jobs, platform triggers) that are neither human nor agent-initiated.
    99→
   100→## Dependencies
   101→- None (foundation story)
   102→
   103→---
   104→
   105→# US-UI-002: Identity Wrapping and Agent Registration
   106→
   107→## Problem
   108→Marcus has existing `person` records for humans and disconnected `agent_session` records with bare string identifiers. After the hub-spoke schema exists (US-UI-001), these existing records need to be wrapped in `identity` hub nodes, and template agent identities need to be registered, so that all downstream edge migration (US-UI-003) has identity records to point at.
   109→
   110→## Who
   111→- Workspace owner | Has existing person records and knows which agent types operate in each workspace | Needs identity records to exist before edges can migrate
   112→
   113→## Solution
   114→Create workspace bootstrap logic that: (1) wraps each existing `person` in an `identity` hub with type "human" and creates the spoke edge, (2) registers template agent identities for each known agent type with managed_by pointing to the workspace owner's identity. This runs as a one-time bootstrap for existing workspaces and as part of workspace creation for new ones.
   115→
   116→## Domain Examples
   117→
   118→### 1: Wrapping Marcus Oliveira -- Existing Human
   119→Person record `person:marcus` exists with `name: "Marcus Oliveira"`, `contact_email: "marcus@conductor.dev"`. The wrapping process creates `identity:marcus-human-san-jose` with `name: "Marcus Oliveira"`, `type: "human"`, `role: "owner"`, and creates an `identity_person` edge connecting them. The person record is not modified.
   120→
   121→### 2: Registering PM Agent Template -- New Agent Identity
   122→No PM Agent identity exists yet. The bootstrap creates `identity:pm-agent-san-jose` with `name: "PM Agent"`, `type: "agent"`, `role: "management"`, creates agent spoke `agent:pm-san-jose` with `agent_type: "management"`, `managed_by: identity:marcus-human-san-jose`, and creates the `identity_agent` edge.
   123→
   124→### 3: Idempotent Bootstrap -- Running Twice
   125→Marcus triggers workspace bootstrap a second time. The wrapping process detects that `identity:marcus-human-san-jose` already exists with an `identity_person` edge to `person:marcus` and skips creation. No duplicate identities are created.
   126→
   127→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   128→
   129→### Scenario: Existing person wrapped in identity hub
   130→Given person "Marcus Oliveira" exists in workspace "san-jose" with contact_email "marcus@conductor.dev"
   131→And no identity record exists for "Marcus Oliveira"
   132→When the identity wrapping bootstrap runs for workspace "san-jose"
   133→Then identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human" and role "owner"
   134→And an identity_person edge connects the identity to person "Marcus Oliveira"
   135→And person "Marcus Oliveira" retains all existing fields unchanged
   136→
   137→### Scenario: Template agent identities registered
   138→Given workspace "san-jose" exists with owner identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   139→And the workspace has agent types ["management", "code_agent", "observer"]
   140→When the agent registration bootstrap runs
   141→Then identity "PM Agent" exists with type "agent" and role "management"
   142→And identity "Code Agent" exists with type "agent" and role "coder"
   143→And identity "Analytics Agent" exists with type "agent" and role "observer"
   144→And each agent spoke has managed_by pointing to identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   145→
   146→### Scenario: Bootstrap is idempotent
   147→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" already exists with an identity_person edge
   148→When the identity wrapping bootstrap runs again for workspace "san-jose"
   149→Then no new identity record is created for "Marcus Oliveira"
   150→And the existing identity_person edge is preserved
   151→And the total identity count for "Marcus Oliveira" remains 1
   152→
   153→### Scenario: Agent managed_by chain resolves to human
   154→Given identity "PM Agent" was registered with managed_by "Marcus Oliveira"
   155→When the managed_by chain is traversed from "PM Agent"
   156→Then the chain reaches identity "Marcus Oliveira" with type "human" in 1 hop
   157→
   158→### Scenario: New workspace creation includes identity bootstrap
   159→Given a new workspace "tokyo-v2" is being created by person "Ana Torres"
   160→When the workspace creation completes
   161→Then identity "Ana Torres" exists with type "human" and role "owner" in workspace "tokyo-v2"
   162→And template agent identities are registered with managed_by "Ana Torres"
   163→
   164→## Acceptance Criteria
   165→- [ ] Every person record in a workspace has a corresponding identity (type: human) with spoke edge
   166→- [ ] Template agent identities created for management, code_agent, and observer types
   167→- [ ] Each agent spoke has managed_by pointing to the workspace owner's human identity
   168→- [ ] Bootstrap is idempotent -- running twice produces no duplicates
   169→- [ ] New workspace creation automatically runs identity bootstrap
   170→- [ ] managed_by chain from any agent resolves to a human identity within bounded hops
   171→
   172→## Technical Notes
   173→- Bootstrap logic belongs in workspace creation flow (`workspace/workspace-routes.ts` or dedicated bootstrap module)
   174→- Existing workspace migration: per project convention, schema changes are breaking and old data is discarded. No backfill of existing person records into identity wrappers. The bootstrap runs on workspace creation; existing workspaces get fresh data on next setup. If a one-time bootstrap for existing workspaces is needed, it can be a versioned migration script applied via `bun migrate`.
   175→- Agent type list is derived from existing `authority_scope` agent_type enum values
   176→- No backwards compatibility needed -- this is additive (new records only, person table unchanged)
   177→
   178→## Dependencies
   179→- US-UI-001 (identity hub schema must exist)

   182→
   183→# US-UI-003: Edge Migration -- Ownership and Attribution
   184→
   185→## Problem
   186→Marcus has identity records for humans and agents (from US-UI-002), but all ownership edges (`owns`, `member_of`) and attribution fields (`task.owner`, `decision.decided_by`, `feature.owner`, `question.assigned_to`) still point at `person` records. The system cannot answer "who owns this?" with a unified identity -- it only sees humans, not agents.
   187→
   188→## Who
   189→- Workspace owner | Needs all graph edges to speak "identity" language | Largest surface area change in the migration
   190→
   191→## Solution
   192→Schema migration that changes all `record<person>` fields to `record<identity>` on task, feature, decision, and question tables. Update `owns` and `member_of` relation table constraints from `IN person` to `IN identity`. Update all TypeScript types and queries that reference `RecordId<"person">` in ownership contexts.
   193→
   194→## Domain Examples
   195→
   196→### 1: Task Ownership Migration -- "Implement OAuth flow"
   197→Task `task:oauth` has `owner: person:marcus`. After migration, the field type changes to `record<identity>` and the value becomes `identity:marcus-human-san-jose`. The PM Agent can now also own tasks: `task:ci-pipeline` gets `owner: identity:pm-agent-san-jose`.
   198→
   199→### 2: Decision Attribution -- "Use hub-and-spoke model"
   200→Decision `decision:hub-spoke` has `decided_by: person:marcus`. After migration, `decided_by` type becomes `record<identity>`. A future decision proposed by the PM Agent would have `decided_by: identity:pm-agent-san-jose`, `confirmed_by: identity:marcus-human-san-jose`.
   201→
   202→### 3: Relation Table Constraint -- owns
   203→The `owns` relation table currently has `TYPE RELATION IN person OUT task | project | feature`. After migration, it becomes `TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT task | project | feature`. Existing edges are recreated with identity records as the IN node.
   204→
   205→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   206→
   207→### Scenario: Task owner field accepts identity record
   208→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human"
   209→When task "Implement OAuth flow" is created with owner identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   210→Then the task is persisted with owner of type record<identity>
   211→And querying the task returns owner identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   212→
   213→### Scenario: Agent identity can own a task
   214→Given identity "PM Agent" exists with type "agent"
   215→When the PM Agent creates task "Set up CI pipeline"
   216→Then the task has owner pointing to identity "PM Agent"
   217→And the owner field type is record<identity> (same type as human-owned tasks)
   218→
   219→### Scenario: Decision has split attribution (proposed by agent, confirmed by human)
   220→Given identity "PM Agent" exists with type "agent"
   221→And identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human"
   222→When decision "Prioritize auth feature" is created with decided_by "PM Agent"
   223→And later confirmed by "Marcus Oliveira"
   224→Then decided_by references identity "PM Agent"
   225→And confirmed_by references identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   226→
   227→### Scenario: owns relation uses identity as source node
   228→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human"
   229→And task "Implement OAuth flow" exists
   230→When an owns edge is created from "Marcus Oliveira" to the task
   231→Then the relation is valid with IN identity OUT task
   232→And traversal SELECT ->owns->task FROM identity:marcus returns the task
   233→
   234→### Scenario: member_of relation connects identity to workspace
   235→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" exists with type "human"
   236→And workspace "san-jose" exists
   237→When a member_of edge connects "Marcus Oliveira" to "san-jose"
   238→Then the relation is valid with IN identity OUT workspace
   239→And traversal SELECT <-member_of<-identity FROM workspace returns "Marcus Oliveira"
   240→
   241→### Scenario: No remaining record<person> in ownership fields
   242→Given the edge migration has completed
   243→When schema info is queried for tables task, feature, decision, question
   244→Then no field has type record<person> for ownership/attribution
   245→And all ownership fields use record<identity>
   246→
   247→## Acceptance Criteria
   248→- [ ] `task.owner` type changed from `record<person>` to `record<identity>`
   249→- [ ] `feature.owner` type changed from `record<person>` to `record<identity>`
   250→- [ ] `decision.decided_by` type changed from `record<person>` to `record<identity>`
   251→- [ ] `decision.confirmed_by` type changed from `record<person>` to `record<identity>`
   252→- [ ] `question.assigned_to` type changed from `record<person>` to `record<identity>`
   253→- [ ] `owns` relation changed to `IN identity OUT task | project | feature`
   254→- [ ] `member_of` relation changed to `IN identity OUT workspace`
   255→- [ ] All TypeScript `RecordId<"person">` usages in ownership contexts changed to `RecordId<"identity">`
   256→- [ ] Graph traversal queries return correct results with identity references
   257→
   258→## Technical Notes
   259→- Schema migration: `DEFINE FIELD OVERWRITE owner ON task TYPE option<record<identity>>;` (using OVERWRITE per convention)
   260→- No data migration needed (project convention: schema changes are breaking, old data discarded)
   261→- TypeScript impact assessment: grep for `RecordId<"person">` and `record<person>` across all server files
   262→- Relation table changes: `ALTER TABLE owns` or `DEFINE TABLE OVERWRITE owns TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT task | project | feature;`
   263→- The `person` table continues to exist as a spoke -- only references FROM other tables TO person are removed for ownership fields
   264→- `extraction/person.ts` still creates person records -- but the identity wrapping (US-UI-002) creates the hub node, and ownership edges point at the hub

   266→## Dependencies
   267→- US-UI-001 (identity table schema)
   268→- US-UI-002 (identity records must exist for edge targets)

   271→
   272→# US-UI-004: Auth Rewiring -- Session and Account Migration
   273→
   274→## Problem
   275→Marcus's login flow (OAuth via GitHub) creates sessions and accounts pointing at `person` records. After edge migration (US-UI-003), the rest of the system speaks "identity" but the auth layer still speaks "person." This creates a type mismatch: the session says "person:marcus" but the task owner says "identity:marcus-human." The system cannot connect "who is logged in" with "who owns this task."
   276→
   277→## Who
   278→- Workspace owner | Authenticates via GitHub OAuth | Must not lose ability to log in during migration
   279→
   280→## Solution
   281→Rename `session.person_id` to `session.identity_id` (type `record<identity>`). Rename `account.person_id` to `account.identity_id`. Update `iam/identity.ts` resolution functions to return `RecordId<"identity">` instead of `RecordId<"person">`. The resolution path becomes: OAuth callback -> find person by email -> find wrapping identity via reverse spoke traversal -> create session with identity reference.
   282→
   283→## Domain Examples
   284→
   285→### 1: OAuth Login for Marcus Oliveira
   286→Marcus clicks "Sign in with GitHub." The OAuth callback returns email "marcus@conductor.dev." The system finds `person:marcus` by email, then traverses the reverse spoke edge (`<-identity_person<-identity`) to find `identity:marcus-human-san-jose`. The session is created with `identity_id: identity:marcus-human-san-jose`.
   287→
   288→### 2: Session Lookup for Chat Message
   289→Marcus sends a chat message. The ingress handler extracts the session token, looks up the session, and gets `identity_id: identity:marcus-human-san-jose`. The chat context is built with this identity as the actor. `humanPresent` is set to `true` because `identity.type = 'human'`.
   290→
   291→### 3: MCP Agent Authentication
   292→The Code Agent connects via MCP protocol. MCP auth resolves the agent's credentials to `identity:code-agent-san-jose` (type: "agent"). The chat context has `humanPresent = false` and `agentType` derived from the identity's role.
   293→
   294→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   295→
   296→### Scenario: OAuth login creates session with identity reference
   297→Given person "Marcus Oliveira" exists with contact_email "marcus@conductor.dev"
   298→And identity "Marcus Oliveira" wraps person "Marcus Oliveira" via identity_person edge
   299→When Marcus authenticates via GitHub OAuth with email "marcus@conductor.dev"
   300→Then a session is created with identity_id pointing to identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   301→And the session does not contain a person_id field
   302→
   303→### Scenario: Session lookup resolves to identity
   304→Given Marcus has an active session with identity_id "Marcus Oliveira"
   305→When the session is looked up by token
   306→Then the result includes identity_id of type record<identity>
   307→And the resolved identity has type "human"
   308→
   309→### Scenario: Chat ingress uses identity from session
   310→Given Marcus has an active session with identity_id "Marcus Oliveira"
   311→When Marcus sends a chat message
   312→Then the chat context actor is identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   313→And humanPresent is true (because identity.type = "human")
   314→
   315→### Scenario: Account table references identity
   316→Given Marcus has a GitHub OAuth account
   317→When the account record is queried
   318→Then account.identity_id points to identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   319→And the account retains provider_id, access_token, and other OAuth fields
   320→
   321→### Scenario: Identity resolution returns identity RecordId
   322→Given person "Marcus Oliveira" exists with contact_email "marcus@conductor.dev"
   323→And identity "Marcus Oliveira" wraps that person
   324→When resolveByEmail("marcus@conductor.dev") is called
   325→Then the return type is RecordId<"identity">
   326→And the returned ID matches identity "Marcus Oliveira"
   327→
   328→## Acceptance Criteria
   329→- [ ] `session.person_id` renamed to `session.identity_id` of type `record<identity>`
   330→- [ ] `account.person_id` renamed to `account.identity_id` of type `record<identity>`
   331→- [ ] `resolveIdentity()` returns `RecordId<"identity">`
   332→- [ ] `resolveByEmail()` returns `RecordId<"identity">`
   333→- [ ] OAuth login flow works end-to-end with identity-based sessions
   334→- [ ] Chat ingress correctly resolves user identity from session
   335→- [ ] `humanPresent` flag derived from `identity.type = 'human'`
   336→
   337→## Technical Notes
   338→- `iam/identity.ts` is the critical file -- resolution functions must traverse person -> identity spoke edge
   339→- Query pattern: `SELECT <-identity_person<-identity FROM person WHERE contact_email = $email`
   340→- Session table migration: `REMOVE FIELD person_id ON session; DEFINE FIELD identity_id ON session TYPE record<identity>;`
   341→- Account table migration: same pattern as session
   342→- Auth adapter (`auth/adapter.ts`) and config (`auth/config.ts`) must be updated to use identity_id
   343→- This is a critical path change -- auth failure means users cannot log in
   344→
   345→## Dependencies
   346→- US-UI-001 (identity table schema)
   347→- US-UI-002 (identity records must exist)
   348→- US-UI-003 (edge migration should complete first so the whole system speaks "identity")

   351→
   352→# US-UI-005: Dual-Label Audit Trail Query
   353→
   354→## Problem
   355→Marcus has unified identities, migrated edges, and rewired auth. But the payoff is missing: he still cannot run the query he most wants -- "show me all suggestions made by agents that were actually implemented." The system has the data but no dedicated query path that returns dual-label attribution (actor + accountable human) for agent actions.
   356→
   357→## Who
   358→- Workspace owner | Evaluating AI ROI | Wants to see which agent suggestions led to real outcomes
   359→
   360→## Solution
   361→Implement audit trail queries that return dual-label attribution for any entity: the immediate actor (human or agent identity) and, for agent actors, the accountable human resolved via `managed_by` chain on the agent spoke. Surface this through the chat agent's entity detail tool and enable the "agent suggestions that became tasks" query.
   362→
   363→## Domain Examples
   364→
   365→### 1: PM Agent Suggestion Implemented -- "Prioritize auth feature"
   366→The PM Agent (identity: "PM Agent", managed_by: Marcus) created suggestion "Prioritize auth feature" 30 days ago. Marcus accepted it, creating task "Implement OAuth flow" which is now status "done." The audit query returns: `{ suggestion: "Prioritize auth", suggested_by: "PM Agent", actor_type: "agent", accountable_human: "Marcus Oliveira", task: "Implement OAuth flow", status: "done" }`.
   367→
   368→### 2: Code Agent Suggestion Pending -- "Add rate limiting"
   369→The Code Agent suggested "Add rate limiting" 20 days ago. The resulting task "Add API rate limiting" is status "in_progress." The audit query includes this with status "in_progress" so Marcus can see which agent suggestions are still being worked on.
   370→
   371→### 3: Human Action -- No Managed-By Chain
   372→Marcus directly created decision "Use SurrealDB for graph." The audit trail shows `actor: "Marcus Oliveira"`, `actor_type: "human"`, `accountable_human: "Marcus Oliveira"` (same person -- no managed_by traversal needed). The dual-label degrades gracefully for human actors.
   373→
   374→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   375→
   376→### Scenario: Query agent suggestions that became implemented tasks
   377→Given PM Agent created suggestion "Prioritize auth feature" 30 days ago
   378→And Marcus accepted it, creating task "Implement OAuth flow" with status "done"
   379→And Code Agent created suggestion "Add rate limiting" 20 days ago
   380→And Marcus accepted it, creating task "Add API rate limiting" with status "in_progress"
   381→When Marcus queries "suggestions by agents that were implemented"
   382→Then the results include "Prioritize auth" by PM Agent linked to task "Implement OAuth flow" (done)
   383→And the results include "Add rate limiting" by Code Agent linked to task "Add API rate limiting" (in_progress)
   384→And each result shows accountable_human "Marcus Oliveira"
   385→
   386→### Scenario: Dual-label attribution for agent action
   387→Given PM Agent created task "Set up CI pipeline"
   388→And PM Agent's managed_by chain resolves to Marcus Oliveira
   389→When the task audit trail is queried
   390→Then the result shows actor "PM Agent" with type "agent"
   391→And the result shows accountable_human "Marcus Oliveira"
   392→And the display format is "Created by PM Agent (Managed by Marcus Oliveira)"
   393→
   394→### Scenario: Human action shows self as accountable
   395→Given Marcus Oliveira directly created decision "Use SurrealDB for graph"
   396→When the decision audit trail is queried
   397→Then the result shows actor "Marcus Oliveira" with type "human"
   398→And accountable_human is "Marcus Oliveira" (same as actor)
   399→
   400→### Scenario: Complete actor history for a task
   401→Given task "Implement OAuth flow" had these actions:
   402→  | action    | actor           | type  |
   403→  | created   | PM Agent        | agent |
   404→  | assigned  | Marcus Oliveira | human |
   405→  | completed | Marcus Oliveira | human |
   406→When the full audit trail for the task is queried
   407→Then all 3 actions are listed chronologically with identity attribution
   408→And agent actions include managed_by human in the response
   409→
   410→### Scenario: No unattributed actions in query results
   411→Given the unified identity migration is complete
   412→When Marcus queries all tasks in workspace "san-jose"
   413→Then every task with an owner shows an identity reference
   414→And no task owner is null or references a person record directly
   415→
   416→## Acceptance Criteria
   417→- [ ] "Agent suggestions that became tasks" query returns results with dual-label attribution
   418→- [ ] Dual-label format includes actor name, actor type, and accountable human (via managed_by)
   419→- [ ] Human actor actions show self as accountable human (graceful degradation)
   420→- [ ] Entity detail tool returns actor identity with type context
   421→- [ ] Query handles mixed human/agent attribution in results
   422→
   423→## Technical Notes
   424→- Query pattern for managed_by resolution: `SELECT ->identity_agent->agent.managed_by AS manager FROM identity WHERE type = 'agent'`
   425→- The "suggestions that became tasks" query depends on existing suggestion -> task graph edges (verify these exist in current schema)
   426→- Chat agent entity detail tool (`chat/tools/get-entity-detail.ts`) needs to return identity type context
   427→- Consider adding a graph query helper for dual-label resolution (reusable across audit queries)
   428→- No new schema changes -- this is a query/code layer change built on top of US-UI-001 through US-UI-004
   429→
   430→## Dependencies
   431→- US-UI-001 through US-UI-004 (all schema and migration work must be complete)

   434→
   435→# US-UI-006: Role-Based Authority with Per-Identity Overrides
   436→
   437→## Problem
   438→Marcus wants to give the PM Agent elevated permissions (e.g., auto-confirm decisions) without changing permissions for all "management" type agents. The current `authority_scope` table uses string-based `agent_type` matching -- all agents of the same type get identical permissions with no way to promote or restrict individual agent instances.
   439→
   440→## Who
   441→- Workspace owner | Managing multiple agent instances of the same type | Needs granular permission control without losing role-based defaults
   442→
   443→## Solution
   444→Modify `authority_scope` to match on `identity.role` instead of `agent_type` string. Add an `authorized_to` relation table for per-identity override edges. Update `checkAuthority()` to check overrides first, then role defaults, then fail-safe blocked.
   445→
   446→## Domain Examples
   447→
   448→### 1: Role-Based Default -- PM Agent Creates Task
   449→Identity "PM Agent" has `role: "management"`. `authority_scope` has row `{ role: "management", action: "create_task", permission: "auto" }`. When PM Agent tries to create a task, `checkAuthority()` matches on role and returns "auto." No override needed.
   450→
   451→### 2: Per-Identity Override -- Lead Coder Confirms Decisions
   452→Identity "Lead Coder" has `role: "coder"`. Role default for "coder" + "confirm_decision" is "provisional." But an `authorized_to` edge exists: `RELATE identity:lead-coder->authorized_to->authority_override:confirm-auto SET permission = "auto"`. When Lead Coder tries to confirm, the override check finds the edge and returns "auto" instead of "provisional."
   453→
   454→### 3: No Role, No Override -- Unknown Agent Blocked
   455→Identity "Unknown Agent" has `role: NONE`. No `authorized_to` edges exist. `checkAuthority()` falls through both checks and returns "blocked." The agent cannot perform any action.
   456→
   457→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
   458→
   459→### Scenario: Role-based permission resolves from identity role
   460→Given identity "PM Agent" has role "management"
   461→And authority_scope has entry: role "management", action "create_task", permission "auto"
   462→When checkAuthority is called for "PM Agent" and action "create_task"
   463→Then permission "auto" is returned
   464→
   465→### Scenario: Per-identity override takes precedence over role
   466→Given identity "Lead Coder" has role "coder"
   467→And authority_scope has: role "coder", action "confirm_decision", permission "provisional"
   468→And authorized_to override grants "Lead Coder" permission "auto" for "confirm_decision"
   469→When checkAuthority is called for "Lead Coder" and action "confirm_decision"
   470→Then permission "auto" is returned from the override
   471→
   472→### Scenario: Role default used when no override exists
   473→Given identity "Junior Coder" has role "coder"
   474→And authority_scope has: role "coder", action "confirm_decision", permission "provisional"
   475→And no authorized_to override exists for "Junior Coder"
   476→When checkAuthority is called for "Junior Coder" and action "confirm_decision"
   477→Then permission "provisional" is returned from the role default
   478→
   479→### Scenario: No role and no override returns blocked
   480→Given identity "Rogue Agent" has no role (role is NONE)
   481→And no authorized_to override edges exist for "Rogue Agent"
   482→When checkAuthority is called for "Rogue Agent" and action "create_observation"
   483→Then permission "blocked" is returned
   484→
   485→### Scenario: Human identity still bypasses authority
   486→Given identity "Marcus Oliveira" has type "human" and role "owner"
   487→And humanPresent is true in the chat context
   488→When Marcus attempts action "confirm_decision"
   489→Then authority check is bypassed entirely
   490→And permission "auto" is returned
   491→
   492→## Acceptance Criteria
   493→- [ ] `authority_scope` matches on `role` field (string) instead of / in addition to `agent_type`
   494→- [ ] `authorized_to` relation table defined: IN identity OUT authority_override (or similar)
   495→- [ ] `checkAuthority()` resolution order: override edge -> role default -> blocked
   496→- [ ] Human identity with humanPresent still bypasses authority (unchanged behavior)
   497→- [ ] Override edges are optional -- system works with role defaults alone
   498→
   499→## Technical Notes
   500→- `authority_scope` table may need a migration to add `role` field or rename `agent_type` to `role`
   501→- `authorized_to` relation: `DEFINE TABLE authorized_to TYPE RELATION IN identity OUT authority_scope;` (or a dedicated override table)
   502→- `checkAuthority()` in `iam/authority.ts` needs a new first step: query `authorized_to` edges for the identity
   503→- Consider workspace-scoped overrides (override edge has workspace field)
   504→- Backwards compatibility: if `agent_type` field is retained alongside `role`, existing seed data continues to work during transition
   505→
   506→## Dependencies
   507→- US-UI-001 (identity table with role field)
   508→- US-UI-002 (agent identities must have roles assigned)
